NKJV.1: A Psalm of David. Do not fret because of evildoers, Nor be envious of the workers of iniquity.
NKJV.2: For they shall soon be cut down like the grass, And wither as the green herb.
NKJV.3: Trust in the LORD, and do good; Dwell in the land, and feed on His faithfulness.
NKJV.4: Delight yourself also in the LORD, And He shall give you the desires of your heart.
NKJV.5: Commit your way to the LORD, Trust also in Him, And He shall bring it to pass.
NKJV.6: He shall bring forth your righteousness as the light, And your justice as the noonday.
NKJV.7: Rest in the LORD, and wait patiently for Him; Do not fret because of him who prospers in his way, Because of the man who brings wicked schemes to pass.
NKJV.8: Cease from anger, and forsake wrath; Do not fret—it only causes harm.
NKJV.9: For evildoers shall be cut off; But those who wait on the LORD, They shall inherit the earth.
NKJV.10: For yet a little while and the wicked shall be no more; Indeed, you will look carefully for his place, But it shall be no more.
NKJV.11: But the meek shall inherit the earth, And shall delight themselves in the abundance of peace.
NKJV.12: The wicked plots against the just,  And gnashes at him with his teeth.
NKJV.13: The Lord laughs at him, For He sees that his day is coming.
NKJV.14: The wicked have drawn the sword And have bent their bow, To cast down the poor and needy, To slay those who are of upright conduct.
NKJV.15: Their sword shall enter their own heart, And their bows shall be broken.
NKJV.16: A little that a righteous man has Is better than the riches of many wicked.
NKJV.17: For the arms of the wicked shall be broken, But the LORD upholds the righteous.
NKJV.18: The LORD knows the days of the upright, And their inheritance shall be forever.
NKJV.19: They shall not be ashamed in the evil time, And in the days of famine they shall be satisfied.
NKJV.20: But the wicked shall perish; And the enemies of the LORD, Like the splendor of the meadows, shall vanish. Into smoke they shall vanish away.
NKJV.21: The wicked borrows and does not repay, But the righteous shows mercy and gives.
NKJV.22: For those blessed by Him shall inherit the earth, But those cursed by Him shall be cut off.
NKJV.23: The steps of a good man are ordered by the LORD, And He delights in his way.
NKJV.24: Though he fall, he shall not be utterly cast down; For the LORD upholds him with His hand.
NKJV.25: I have been young, and now am old; Yet I have not seen the righteous forsaken, Nor his descendants begging bread.
NKJV.26: He is ever merciful, and lends; And his descendants are blessed.
NKJV.27: Depart from evil, and do good; And dwell forevermore.
NKJV.28: For the LORD loves justice, And does not forsake His saints; They are preserved forever, But the descendants of the wicked shall be cut off.
NKJV.29: The righteous shall inherit the land, And dwell in it forever.
NKJV.30: The mouth of the righteous speaks wisdom, And his tongue talks of justice.
NKJV.31: The law of his God is in his heart; None of his steps shall slide.
NKJV.32: The wicked watches the righteous, And seeks to slay him.
NKJV.33: The LORD will not leave him in his hand, Nor condemn him when he is judged.
NKJV.34: Wait on the LORD, And keep His way, And He shall exalt you to inherit the land; When the wicked are cut off, you shall see it.
NKJV.35: I have seen the wicked in great power, And spreading himself like a native green tree.
NKJV.36: Yet he passed away, and behold, he was no more; Indeed I sought him, but he could not be found.
NKJV.37: Mark the blameless man, and observe the upright; For the future of that man is peace.
NKJV.38: But the transgressors shall be destroyed together; The future of the wicked shall be cut off.
NKJV.39: But the salvation of the righteous is from the LORD; He is their strength in the time of trouble.
NKJV.40: And the LORD shall help them and deliver them; He shall deliver them from the wicked, And save them,  Because they trust in Him.

ESV.1: Fret not yourself because of evildoers; be not envious of wrongdoers!
ESV.2: For they will soon fade like the grass and wither like the green herb.
ESV.3: Trust in the LORD, and do good; dwell in the land and befriend faithfulness.
ESV.4: Delight yourself in the LORD, and he will give you the desires of your heart.
ESV.5: Commit your way to the LORD; trust in him, and he will act.
ESV.6: He will bring forth your righteousness as the light, and your justice as the noonday.
ESV.7: Be still before the LORD and wait patiently for him; fret not yourself over the one who prospers in his way, over the man who carries out evil devices!
ESV.8: Refrain from anger, and forsake wrath! Fret not yourself; it tends only to evil.
ESV.9: For the evildoers shall be cut off, but those who wait for the LORD shall inherit the land.
ESV.10: In just a little while, the wicked will be no more; though you look carefully at his place, he will not be there.
ESV.11: But the meek shall inherit the land and delight themselves in abundant peace.
ESV.12: The wicked plots against the righteous and gnashes his teeth at him,
ESV.13: but the Lord laughs at the wicked, for he sees that his day is coming.
ESV.14: The wicked draw the sword and bend their bows to bring down the poor and needy, to slay those whose way is upright;
ESV.15: their sword shall enter their own heart, and their bows shall be broken.
ESV.16: Better is the little that the righteous has than the abundance of many wicked.
ESV.17: For the arms of the wicked shall be broken, but the LORD upholds the righteous.
ESV.18: The LORD knows the days of the blameless, and their heritage will remain forever;
ESV.19: they are not put to shame in evil times; in the days of famine they have abundance.
ESV.20: But the wicked will perish; the enemies of the LORD are like the glory of the pastures; they vanish — like smoke they vanish away.
ESV.21: The wicked borrows but does not pay back, but the righteous is generous and gives;
ESV.22: for those blessed by the LORD  shall inherit the land, but those cursed by him shall be cut off.
ESV.23: The steps of a man are established by the LORD, when he delights in his way;
ESV.24: though he fall, he shall not be cast headlong, for the LORD upholds his hand.
ESV.25: I have been young, and now am old, yet I have not seen the righteous forsaken or his children begging for bread.
ESV.26: He is ever lending generously, and his children become a blessing.
ESV.27: Turn away from evil and do good; so shall you dwell forever.
ESV.28: For the LORD loves justice; he will not forsake his saints. They are preserved forever, but the children of the wicked shall be cut off.
ESV.29: The righteous shall inherit the land and dwell upon it forever.
ESV.30: The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom, and his tongue speaks justice.
ESV.31: The law of his God is in his heart; his steps do not slip.
ESV.32: The wicked watches for the righteous and seeks to put him to death.
ESV.33: The LORD will not abandon him to his power or let him be condemned when he is brought to trial.
ESV.34: Wait for the LORD and keep his way, and he will exalt you to inherit the land; you will look on when the wicked are cut off.
ESV.35: I have seen a wicked, ruthless man, spreading himself like a green laurel tree.
ESV.36: But he passed away,  and behold, he was no more; though I sought him, he could not be found.
ESV.37: Mark the blameless and behold the upright, for there is a future for the man of peace.
ESV.38: But transgressors shall be altogether destroyed; the future of the wicked shall be cut off.
ESV.39: The salvation of the righteous is from the LORD; he is their stronghold in the time of trouble.
ESV.40: The LORD helps them and delivers them; he delivers them from the wicked and saves them, because they take refuge in him.

NASB.1: [A Psalm] of David. Do not fret because of evildoers, Be not envious toward wrongdoers.
NASB.2: For they will wither quickly like the grass And fade like the green herb.
NASB.3: Trust in the LORD and do good; Dwell in the land and cultivate faithfulness.
NASB.4: Delight yourself in the LORD; And He will give you the desires of your heart.
NASB.5: Commit your way to the LORD, Trust also in Him, and He will do it.
NASB.6: He will bring forth your righteousness as the light And your judgment as the noonday.
NASB.7: Rest in the LORD and wait patiently for Him; Do not fret because of him who prospers in his way, Because of the man who carries out wicked schemes.
NASB.8: Cease from anger and forsake wrath; Do not fret; [it leads] only to evildoing.
NASB.9: For evildoers will be cut off, But those who wait for the LORD, they will inherit the land.
NASB.10: Yet a little while and the wicked man will be no more; And you will look carefully for his place and he will not be [there].
NASB.11: But the humble will inherit the land And will delight themselves in abundant prosperity.
NASB.12: The wicked plots against the righteous And gnashes at him with his teeth.
NASB.13: The Lord laughs at him, For He sees his day is coming.
NASB.14: The wicked have drawn the sword and bent their bow To cast down the afflicted and the needy, To slay those who are upright in conduct.
NASB.15: Their sword will enter their own heart, And their bows will be broken.
NASB.16: Better is the little of the righteous Than the abundance of many wicked.
NASB.17: For the arms of the wicked will be broken, But the LORD sustains the righteous.
NASB.18: The LORD knows the days of the blameless, And their inheritance will be forever.
NASB.19: They will not be ashamed in the time of evil, And in the days of famine they will have abundance.
NASB.20: But the wicked will perish; And the enemies of the LORD will be like the glory of the pastures, They vanish-- like smoke they vanish away.
NASB.21: The wicked borrows and does not pay back, But the righteous is gracious and gives.
NASB.22: For those blessed by Him will inherit the land, But those cursed by Him will be cut off.
NASB.23: The steps of a man are established by the LORD, And He delights in his way.
NASB.24: When he falls, he will not be hurled headlong, Because the LORD is the One who holds his hand.
NASB.25: I have been young and now I am old, Yet I have not seen the righteous forsaken Or his descendants begging bread.
NASB.26: All day long he is gracious and lends, And his descendants are a blessing.
NASB.27: Depart from evil and do good, So you will abide forever.
NASB.28: For the LORD loves justice And does not forsake His godly ones; They are preserved forever, But the descendants of the wicked will be cut off.
NASB.29: The righteous will inherit the land And dwell in it forever.
NASB.30: The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom, And his tongue speaks justice.
NASB.31: The law of his God is in his heart; His steps do not slip.
NASB.32: The wicked spies upon the righteous And seeks to kill him.
NASB.33: The LORD will not leave him in his hand Or let him be condemned when he is judged.
NASB.34: Wait for the LORD and keep His way, And He will exalt you to inherit the land; When the wicked are cut off, you will see it.
NASB.35: I have seen a wicked, violent man Spreading himself like a luxuriant tree in its native soil.
NASB.36: Then he passed away, and lo, he was no more; I sought for him, but he could not be found.
NASB.37: Mark the blameless man, and behold the upright; For the man of peace will have a posterity.
NASB.38: But transgressors will be altogether destroyed; The posterity of the wicked will be cut off.
NASB.39: But the salvation of the righteous is from the LORD; He is their strength in time of trouble.
NASB.40: The LORD helps them and delivers them; He delivers them from the wicked and saves them, Because they take refuge in Him.

NLT.1:
Don’t worry about the wicked
or envy those who do wrong.
NLT.2:
For like grass, they soon fade away.
Like spring flowers, they soon wither.
NLT.3:
Trust in the LORD and do good.
Then you will live safely in the land and prosper.
NLT.4:
Take delight in the LORD,
and he will give you your heart’s desires.
NLT.5:
Commit everything you do to the LORD.
Trust him, and he will help you.
NLT.6:
He will make your innocence radiate like the dawn,
and the justice of your cause will shine like the noonday sun.
NLT.7:
Be still in the presence of the LORD,
and wait patiently for him to act.
Don’t worry about evil people who prosper
or fret about their wicked schemes.
NLT.8:
Stop being angry!
Turn from your rage!
Do not lose your temper —
it only leads to harm.
NLT.9:
For the wicked will be destroyed,
but those who trust in the LORD will possess the land.
NLT.10:
Soon the wicked will disappear.
Though you look for them, they will be gone.
NLT.11:
The lowly will possess the land
and will live in peace and prosperity.
NLT.12:
The wicked plot against the godly;
they snarl at them in defiance.
NLT.13:
But the Lord just laughs,
for he sees their day of judgment coming.
NLT.14:
The wicked draw their swords
and string their bows
to kill the poor and the oppressed,
to slaughter those who do right.
NLT.15:
But their swords will stab their own hearts,
and their bows will be broken.
NLT.16:
It is better to be godly and have little
than to be evil and rich.
NLT.17:
For the strength of the wicked will be shattered,
but the LORD takes care of the godly.
NLT.18:
Day by day the LORD takes care of the innocent,
and they will receive an inheritance that lasts forever.
NLT.19:
They will not be disgraced in hard times;
even in famine they will have more than enough.
NLT.20:
But the wicked will die.
The LORD’s enemies are like flowers in a field —
they will disappear like smoke.
NLT.21:
The wicked borrow and never repay,
but the godly are generous givers.
NLT.22:
Those the LORD blesses will possess the land,
but those he curses will die.
NLT.23:
The LORD directs the steps of the godly.
He delights in every detail of their lives.
NLT.24:
Though they stumble, they will never fall,
for the LORD holds them by the hand.
NLT.25:
Once I was young, and now I am old.
Yet I have never seen the godly abandoned
or their children begging for bread.
NLT.26:
The godly always give generous loans to others,
and their children are a blessing.
NLT.27:
Turn from evil and do good,
and you will live in the land forever.
NLT.28:
For the LORD loves justice,
and he will never abandon the godly.
He will keep them safe forever,
but the children of the wicked will die.
NLT.29:
The godly will possess the land
and will live there forever.
NLT.30:
The godly offer good counsel;
they teach right from wrong.
NLT.31:
They have made God’s law their own,
so they will never slip from his path.
NLT.32:
The wicked wait in ambush for the godly,
looking for an excuse to kill them.
NLT.33:
But the LORD will not let the wicked succeed
or let the godly be condemned when they are put on trial.
NLT.34:
Put your hope in the LORD.
Travel steadily along his path.
He will honor you by giving you the land.
You will see the wicked destroyed.
NLT.35:
I have seen wicked and ruthless people
flourishing like a tree in its native soil.
NLT.36:
But when I looked again, they were gone!
Though I searched for them, I could not find them!
NLT.37:
Look at those who are honest and good,
for a wonderful future awaits those who love peace.
NLT.38:
But the rebellious will be destroyed;
they have no future.
NLT.39:
The LORD rescues the godly;
he is their fortress in times of trouble.
NLT.40:
The LORD helps them,
rescuing them from the wicked.
He saves them,
and they find shelter in him.

WEB.1: By David. Don’t fret because of evildoers, neither be envious against those who work unrighteousness.
WEB.2: For they shall soon be cut down like the grass, and wither like the green herb.
WEB.3: Trust in Yahweh, and do good. Dwell in the land, and enjoy safe pasture.
WEB.4: Also delight yourself in Yahweh, and he will give you the desires of your heart.
WEB.5: Commit your way to Yahweh. Trust also in him, and he will do this:
WEB.6: he will make your righteousness go out as the light, and your justice as the noon day sun.
WEB.7: Rest in Yahweh, and wait patiently for him. Don’t fret because of him who prospers in his way, because of the man who makes wicked plots happen.
WEB.8: Cease from anger, and forsake wrath. Don’t fret, it leads only to evildoing.
WEB.9: For evildoers shall be cut off, but those who wait for Yahweh shall inherit the land.
WEB.10: For yet a little while, and the wicked will be no more. Yes, though you look for his place, he isn’t there.
WEB.11: But the humble shall inherit the land, and shall delight themselves in the abundance of peace.
WEB.12: The wicked plots against the just, and gnashes at him with his teeth.
WEB.13: The Lord will laugh at him, for he sees that his day is coming.
WEB.14: The wicked have drawn out the sword, and have bent their bow, to cast down the poor and needy, to kill those who are upright on the path.
WEB.15: Their sword shall enter into their own heart. Their bows shall be broken.
WEB.16: Better is a little that the righteous has, than the abundance of many wicked.
WEB.17: For the arms of the wicked shall be broken, but Yahweh upholds the righteous.
WEB.18: Yahweh knows the days of the perfect. Their inheritance shall be forever.
WEB.19: They shall not be disappointed in the time of evil. In the days of famine they shall be satisfied.
WEB.20: But the wicked shall perish. The enemies of Yahweh shall be like the beauty of the fields. They will vanish — vanish like smoke.
WEB.21: The wicked borrow, and don’t pay back, but the righteous give generously.
WEB.22: For such as are blessed by him shall inherit the land. Those who are cursed by him shall be cut off.
WEB.23: A man’s goings are established by Yahweh. He delights in his way.
WEB.24: Though he stumble, he shall not fall, for Yahweh holds him up with his hand.
WEB.25: I have been young, and now am old, yet I have not seen the righteous forsaken, nor his children begging for bread.
WEB.26: All day long he deals graciously, and lends. His offspring  is blessed.
WEB.27: Depart from evil, and do good. Live securely forever.
WEB.28: For Yahweh loves justice, and doesn’t forsake his saints. They are preserved forever, but the children of the wicked shall be cut off.
WEB.29: The righteous shall inherit the land, and live in it forever.
WEB.30: The mouth of the righteous talks of wisdom. His tongue speaks justice.
WEB.31: The law of his God is in his heart. None of his steps shall slide.
WEB.32: The wicked watches the righteous, and seeks to kill him.
WEB.33: Yahweh will not leave him in his hand, nor condemn him when he is judged.
WEB.34: Wait for Yahweh, and keep his way, and he will exalt you to inherit the land. When the wicked are cut off, you shall see it.
WEB.35: I have seen the wicked in great power, spreading himself like a green tree in its native soil.
WEB.36: But he passed away, and behold, he was not. Yes, I sought him, but he could not be found.
WEB.37: Mark the perfect man, and see the upright, for there is a future for the man of peace.
WEB.38: As for transgressors, they shall be destroyed together. The future of the wicked shall be cut off.
WEB.39: But the salvation of the righteous is from Yahweh. He is their stronghold in the time of trouble.
WEB.40: Yahweh helps them, and rescues them. He rescues them from the wicked, and saves them, Because they have taken refuge in him.

CSB17.1:
Of David. [1] Do not be agitated by evildoers;
do not envy those who do wrong. ⓕ
CSB17.2:
For they wither quickly like grass
and wilt like tender green plants. ⓖ
CSB17.3:
Trust in the LORD and do what is good; ⓗ
dwell in the land and live securely. [1] ⓘ
CSB17.4:
Take delight in the LORD,
and he will give you your heart’s desires. ⓙ
CSB17.5:
Commit your way to the LORD;
trust in him, and he will act, ⓚ
CSB17.6:
making your righteousness shine like the dawn,
your justice like the noonday. ⓛ
CSB17.7:
Be silent before the LORD and wait expectantly for him; ⓜ
do not be agitated by one who prospers in his way,
by the person who carries out evil plans. ⓝ
CSB17.8:
Refrain from anger and give up your rage;
do not be agitated — it can only bring harm. ⓐ
CSB17.9:
For evildoers will be destroyed,
but those who put their hope in the LORD
will inherit the land. ⓑ
CSB17.10:
A little while, and the wicked person will be no more;
though you look for him, he will not be there. ⓒ
CSB17.11:
But the humble will inherit the land ⓓ
and will enjoy abundant prosperity. ⓔ
CSB17.12:
The wicked person schemes against the righteous
and gnashes his teeth at him. ⓕ
CSB17.13:
The Lord laughs at him ⓖ
because he sees that his day is coming. ⓗ
CSB17.14:
The wicked have drawn the sword and strung the [2] bow
to bring down the poor and needy
and to slaughter those whose way is upright. ⓘ
CSB17.15:
Their swords will enter their own hearts,
and their bows will be broken. ⓙ
CSB17.16:
The little that the righteous person has is better
than the abundance of many wicked people. ⓚ
CSB17.17:
For the arms of the wicked will be broken, ⓛ
but the LORD supports the righteous. ⓜ
CSB17.18:
The LORD watches over the blameless all their days,
and their inheritance will last forever. ⓝ
CSB17.19:
They will not be disgraced in times of adversity;
they will be satisfied in days of hunger. ⓐ
CSB17.20:
But the wicked will perish;
the LORD’s enemies, like the glory of the pastures,
will fade away —
they will fade away like smoke. ⓑ
CSB17.21:
The wicked person borrows and does not repay,
but the righteous one is gracious and giving. ⓒ
CSB17.22:
Those who are blessed by the LORD will inherit the land,
but those cursed by him will be destroyed. ⓓ
CSB17.23:
A person’s steps are established by the LORD,
and he takes pleasure in his way. ⓔ
CSB17.24:
Though he falls, he will not be overwhelmed,
because the LORD supports him with his hand. ⓕ
CSB17.25:
I have been young and now I am old,
yet I have not seen the righteous abandoned
or his children begging for bread. ⓖ
CSB17.26:
He is always generous, always lending,
and his children are a blessing. ⓗ
CSB17.27:
Turn away from evil, do what is good,
and settle permanently. ⓘ
CSB17.28:
For the LORD loves justice
and will not abandon his faithful ones. ⓙ
They are kept safe forever,
but the children of the wicked will be destroyed. ⓚ
CSB17.29:
The righteous will inherit the land
and dwell in it permanently. ⓛ
CSB17.30:
The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom;
his tongue speaks what is just. ⓜ
CSB17.31:
The instruction of his God is in his heart;
his steps do not falter. ⓝ
CSB17.32:
The wicked one lies in wait for the righteous
and intends to kill him; ⓐ
CSB17.33:
the LORD will not leave him
in the power of the wicked one
or allow him to be condemned when he is judged. ⓑ
CSB17.34:
Wait for the LORD and keep his way,
and he will exalt you to inherit the land.
You will watch when the wicked are destroyed. ⓒ
CSB17.35:
I have seen a wicked, violent person
well-rooted, [3] like a flourishing native tree. ⓓ
CSB17.36:
Then I [4] passed by and noticed he was gone;
I searched for him, but he could not be found. ⓔ
CSB17.37:
Watch the blameless and observe the upright,
for the person of peace will have a future. [5] ⓕ
CSB17.38:
But transgressors will all be eliminated;
the future of the wicked will be destroyed. ⓖ
CSB17.39:
The salvation of the righteous is from the LORD,
their refuge in a time of distress. ⓗ
CSB17.40:
The LORD helps and delivers them;
he will deliver them from the wicked and will save them
because they take refuge in him. ⓘ

AMP.1:
A Psalm of David. Do not worry because of evildoers,
Nor be envious toward wrongdoers;
AMP.2:
For they will wither quickly like the grass,
And fade like the green herb.
AMP.3:
Trust [rely on and have confidence] in the LORD and do good;
Dwell in the land and feed [securely] on His faithfulness.
AMP.4:
Delight yourself in the LORD,
And He will give you the desires and petitions of your heart.
AMP.5:
Commit your way to the LORD;
Trust in Him also and He will do it.
AMP.6:
He will make your righteousness [your pursuit of right standing with God] like the light,
And your judgment like [the shining of] the noonday [sun].
AMP.7:
Be still before the LORD; wait patiently for Him and entrust yourself to Him;
Do not fret (whine, agonize) because of him who prospers in his way,
Because of the man who carries out wicked schemes.
AMP.8:
Cease from anger and abandon wrath;
Do not fret; it leads only to evil.
AMP.9:
For those who do evil will be cut off,
But those who wait for the LORD, they will inherit the land.
AMP.10:
For yet a little while and the wicked one will be gone [forever];
Though you look carefully where he used to be, he will not be [found].
AMP.11:
But the humble will [at last] inherit the land
And will delight themselves in abundant prosperity and peace.
AMP.12:
The wicked plots against the righteous
And gnashes at him with his teeth.
AMP.13:
The Lord laughs at him [the wicked one—the one who oppresses the righteous],
For He sees that his day [of defeat] is coming.
AMP.14:
The wicked have drawn the sword and bent their bow
To cast down the afflicted and the needy,
To slaughter those who are upright in conduct [those with personal integrity and godly character].
AMP.15:
The sword [of the ungodly] will enter their own heart,
And their bow will be broken.
AMP.16:
Better is the little of the righteous [who seek the will of God]
Than the abundance (riches) of many wicked (godless).
AMP.17:
For the arms of the wicked will be broken,
But the LORD upholds and sustains the righteous [who seek Him].
AMP.18:
The LORD knows the days of the blameless,
And their inheritance will continue forever.
AMP.19:
They will not be ashamed in the time of evil,
And in the days of famine they will have plenty and be satisfied.
AMP.20:
But the wicked (ungodly) will perish,
And the enemies of the LORD will be like the glory of the pastures and like the fat of lambs [that is consumed in smoke],
They vanish—like smoke they vanish away.
AMP.21:
The wicked borrows and does not pay back,
But the righteous is gracious and kind and gives.
AMP.22:
For those blessed by God will [at last] inherit the land,
But those cursed by Him will be cut off.
AMP.23:
The steps of a [good and righteous] man are directed and established by the LORD,
And He delights in his way [and blesses his path].
AMP.24:
When he falls, he will not be hurled down,
Because the LORD is the One who holds his hand and sustains him.
AMP.25:
I have been young and now I am old,
Yet I have not seen the righteous (those in right standing with God) abandoned
Or his descendants pleading for bread.
AMP.26:
All day long he is gracious and lends,
And his descendants are a blessing.
AMP.27:
Depart from evil and do good;
And you will dwell [securely in the land] forever.
AMP.28:
For the LORD delights in justice
And does not abandon His saints (faithful ones);
They are preserved forever,
But the descendants of the wicked will [in time] be cut off.
AMP.29:
The righteous will inherit the land
And live in it forever.
AMP.30:
The mouth of the righteous proclaims wisdom,
And his tongue speaks justice and truth.
AMP.31:
The law of his God is in his heart;
Not one of his steps will slip.
AMP.32:
The wicked lies in wait for the righteous
And seeks to kill him.
AMP.33:
The LORD will not leave him in his hand
Or let him be condemned when he is judged.
AMP.34:
Wait for and expect the LORD and keep His way,
And He will exalt you to inherit the land;
[In the end] when the wicked are cut off, you will see it.
AMP.35:
I have seen a wicked, violent man [with great power]
Spreading and flaunting himself like a cedar in its native soil,
AMP.36:
Yet he passed away, and lo, he was no more;
I sought him, but he could not be found.
AMP.37:
Mark the blameless man [who is spiritually complete], and behold the upright [who walks in moral integrity];
There is a [good] future for the man of peace [because a life of honor blesses one's descendants].
AMP.38:
As for transgressors, they will be completely destroyed;
The future of the wicked will be cut off.
AMP.39:
But the salvation of the righteous is from the LORD;
He is their refuge and stronghold in the time of trouble.
AMP.40:
The LORD helps them and rescues them;
He rescues them from the wicked and saves them,
Because they take refuge in Him.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Don't bother your head with braggarts
or wish you could succeed like the wicked.
MSG.2:
In no time they'll shrivel like grass clippings
and wilt like cut flowers in the sun.
MSG.3:
Get insurance with God and do a good deed,
settle down and stick to your last.
MSG.4:
Keep company with God,
get in on the best.
MSG.5:
Open up before God, keep nothing back;
he'll do whatever needs to be done:
MSG.6:
He'll validate your life in the clear light of day
and stamp you with approval at high noon.
MSG.7:
Quiet down before God,
be prayerful before him.
Don't bother with those who climb the ladder,
who elbow their way to the top.
MSG.8:
Bridle your anger, trash your wrath,
cool your pipes—it only makes things worse.
MSG.9:
Before long the crooks will be bankrupt;
God-investors will soon own the store.
MSG.10:
Before you know it, the wicked will have had it;
you'll stare at his once famous place and—nothing!
MSG.11:
Down-to-earth people will move in and take over,
relishing a huge bonanza.
MSG.12:
Bad guys have it in for the good guys,
obsessed with doing them in.
MSG.13:
But God isn't losing any sleep; to him
they're a joke with no punch line.
MSG.14:
Bullies brandish their swords,
pull back on their bows with a flourish.
They're out to beat up on the harmless,
or mug that nice man out walking his dog.
MSG.15:
A banana peel lands them flat on their faces—
slapstick figures in a moral circus.
MSG.16:
Less is more and more is less.
One righteous will outclass fifty wicked,
MSG.17:
For the wicked are moral weaklings
but the righteous are God-strong.
MSG.18:
God keeps track of the decent folk;
what they do won't soon be forgotten.
MSG.19:
In hard times, they'll hold their heads high;
when the shelves are bare, they'll be full.
MSG.20:
God-despisers have had it;
God's enemies are finished—
Stripped bare like vineyards at harvest time,
vanished like smoke in thin air.
MSG.21:
Wicked borrows and never returns;
Righteous gives and gives.
MSG.22:
Generous gets it all in the end;
Stingy is cut off at the pass.
MSG.23:
Stalwart walks in step with God;
his path blazed by God, he's happy.
MSG.24:
If he stumbles, he's not down for long;
God has a grip on his hand.
MSG.25:
I once was young, now I'm a graybeard—
not once have I seen an abandoned believer,
or his kids out roaming the streets.
MSG.26:
Every day he's out giving and lending,
his children making him proud.
MSG.27:
Turn your back on evil,
work for the good and don't quit.
MSG.28:
God loves this kind of thing,
never turns away from his friends.
Live this way and you've got it made,
but bad eggs will be tossed out.
MSG.29:
The good get planted on good land
and put down healthy roots.
MSG.30:
Righteous chews on wisdom like a dog on a bone,
rolls virtue around on his tongue.
MSG.31:
His heart pumps God's Word like blood through his veins;
his feet are as sure as a cat's.
MSG.32:
Wicked sets a watch for Righteous,
he's out for the kill.
MSG.33:
God, alert, is also on watch—
Wicked won't hurt a hair of his head.
MSG.34:
Wait passionately for God,
don't leave the path.
He'll give you your place in the sun
while you watch the wicked lose it.
MSG.35:
I saw Wicked bloated like a toad,
croaking pretentious nonsense.
MSG.36:
The next time I looked there was nothing—
a punctured bladder, vapid and limp.
MSG.37:
Keep your eye on the healthy soul,
scrutinize the straight life;
There's a future
in strenuous wholeness.
MSG.38:
But the willful will soon be discarded;
insolent souls are on a dead-end street.
MSG.39:
The spacious, free life is from God,
it's also protected and safe.
MSG.40:
God-strengthened, we're delivered from evil—
when we run to him, he saves us.

NIV.1:
Psalm 37
Of David.
Do not fret because of evil men
or be envious of those who do wrong;
NIV.2:
for like the grass they will soon wither,
like green plants they will soon die away.
NIV.3:
Trust in the Lord and do good;
dwell in the land and enjoy safe pasture.
NIV.4:
Delight yourself in the Lord
and he will give you the desires of your heart.
NIV.5:
Commit your way to the Lord;
trust in him and he will do this:
NIV.6:
He will make your righteousness shine like the dawn,
the justice of your cause like the noonday sun.
NIV.7:
Be still before the Lord and wait patiently for him;
do not fret when men succeed in their ways,
when they carry out their wicked schemes.
NIV.8:
Refrain from anger and turn from wrath;
do not fret—it leads only to evil.
NIV.9:
For evil men will be cut off,
but those who hope in the Lord will inherit the land.
NIV.10:
A little while, and the wicked will be no more;
though you look for them, they will not be found.
NIV.11:
But the meek will inherit the land
and enjoy great peace.
NIV.12:
The wicked plot against the righteous
and gnash their teeth at them;
NIV.13:
but the Lord laughs at the wicked,
for he knows their day is coming.
NIV.14:
The wicked draw the sword
and bend the bow
to bring down the poor and needy,
to slay those whose ways are upright.
NIV.15:
But their swords will pierce their own hearts,
and their bows will be broken.
NIV.16:
Better the little that the righteous have
than the wealth of many wicked;
NIV.17:
for the power of the wicked will be broken,
but the Lord upholds the righteous.
NIV.18:
The days of the blameless are known to the Lord,
and their inheritance will endure forever.
NIV.19:
In times of disaster they will not wither;
in days of famine they will enjoy plenty.
NIV.20:
But the wicked will perish:
The Lord’s enemies will be like the beauty of the fields,
they will vanish—vanish like smoke.
NIV.21:
The wicked borrow and do not repay,
but the righteous give generously;
NIV.22:
those the Lord blesses will inherit the land,
but those he curses will be cut off.
NIV.23:
If the Lord delights in a man’s way,
he makes his steps firm;
NIV.24:
though he stumble, he will not fall,
for the Lord upholds him with his hand.
NIV.25:
I was young and now I am old,
yet I have never seen the righteous forsaken
or their children begging bread.
NIV.26:
They are always generous and lend freely;
their children will be blessed.
NIV.27:
Turn from evil and do good;
then you will dwell in the land forever.
NIV.28:
For the Lord loves the just
and will not forsake his faithful ones.
They will be protected forever,
but the offspring of the wicked will be cut off;
NIV.29:
the righteous will inherit the land
and dwell in it forever.
NIV.30:
The mouth of the righteous man utters wisdom,
and his tongue speaks what is just.
NIV.31:
The law of his God is in his heart;
his feet do not slip.
NIV.32:
The wicked lie in wait for the righteous,
seeking their very lives;
NIV.33:
but the Lord will not leave them in their power
or let them be condemned when brought to trial.
NIV.34:
Wait for the Lord
and keep his way.
He will exalt you to inherit the land;
when the wicked are cut off, you will see it.
NIV.35:
I have seen a wicked and ruthless man
flourishing like a green tree in its native soil,
NIV.36:
but he soon passed away and was no more;
though I looked for him, he could not be found.
NIV.37:
Consider the blameless, observe the upright;
there is a future for the man of peace.
NIV.38:
But all sinners will be destroyed;
the future of the wicked will be cut off.
NIV.39:
The salvation of the righteous comes from the Lord;
he is their stronghold in time of trouble.
NIV.40:
The Lord helps them and delivers them;
he delivers them from the wicked and saves them,
because they take refuge in him.

NRSVCE.1:
Do not fret because of the wicked;
do not be envious of wrongdoers,
NRSVCE.2:
for they will soon fade like the grass,
and wither like the green herb.
NRSVCE.3:
Trust in the LORD, and do good;
so you will live in the land, and enjoy security.
NRSVCE.4:
Take delight in the LORD,
and he will give you the desires of your heart.
NRSVCE.5:
Commit your way to the LORD;
trust in him, and he will act.
NRSVCE.6:
He will make your vindication shine like the light,
and the justice of your cause like the noonday.
NRSVCE.7:
Be still before the LORD, and wait patiently for him;
do not fret over those who prosper in their way,
over those who carry out evil devices.
NRSVCE.8:
Refrain from anger, and forsake wrath.
Do not fret — it leads only to evil.
NRSVCE.9:
For the wicked shall be cut off,
but those who wait for the LORD shall inherit the land.
NRSVCE.10:
Yet a little while, and the wicked will be no more;
though you look diligently for their place, they will not be there.
NRSVCE.11:
But the meek shall inherit the land,
and delight themselves in abundant prosperity.
NRSVCE.12:
The wicked plot against the righteous,
and gnash their teeth at them;
NRSVCE.13:
but the LORD laughs at the wicked,
for he sees that their day is coming.
NRSVCE.14:
The wicked draw the sword and bend their bows
to bring down the poor and needy,
to kill those who walk uprightly;
NRSVCE.15:
their sword shall enter their own heart,
and their bows shall be broken.
NRSVCE.16:
Better is a little that the righteous person has
than the abundance of many wicked.
NRSVCE.17:
For the arms of the wicked shall be broken,
but the LORD upholds the righteous.
NRSVCE.18:
The LORD knows the days of the blameless,
and their heritage will abide forever;
NRSVCE.19:
they are not put to shame in evil times,
in the days of famine they have abundance.
NRSVCE.20:
But the wicked perish,
and the enemies of the LORD are like the glory of the pastures;
they vanish — like smoke they vanish away.
NRSVCE.21:
The wicked borrow, and do not pay back,
but the righteous are generous and keep giving;
NRSVCE.22:
for those blessed by the LORD shall inherit the land,
but those cursed by him shall be cut off.
NRSVCE.23:
Our steps are made firm by the LORD,
when he delights in our way;
NRSVCE.24:
though we stumble, we shall not fall headlong,
for the LORD holds us by the hand.
NRSVCE.25:
I have been young, and now am old,
yet I have not seen the righteous forsaken
or their children begging bread.
NRSVCE.26:
They are ever giving liberally and lending,
and their children become a blessing.
NRSVCE.27:
Depart from evil, and do good;
so you shall abide forever.
NRSVCE.28:
For the LORD loves justice;
he will not forsake his faithful ones.
The righteous shall be kept safe forever,
but the children of the wicked shall be cut off.
NRSVCE.29:
The righteous shall inherit the land,
and live in it forever.
NRSVCE.30:
The mouths of the righteous utter wisdom,
and their tongues speak justice.
NRSVCE.31:
The law of their God is in their hearts;
their steps do not slip.
NRSVCE.32:
The wicked watch for the righteous,
and seek to kill them.
NRSVCE.33:
The LORD will not abandon them to their power,
or let them be condemned when they are brought to trial.
NRSVCE.34:
Wait for the LORD, and keep to his way,
and he will exalt you to inherit the land;
you will look on the destruction of the wicked.
NRSVCE.35:
I have seen the wicked oppressing,
and towering like a cedar of Lebanon.
NRSVCE.36:
Again I passed by, and they were no more;
though I sought them, they could not be found.
NRSVCE.37:
Mark the blameless, and behold the upright,
for there is posterity for the peaceable.
NRSVCE.38:
But transgressors shall be altogether destroyed;
the posterity of the wicked shall be cut off.
NRSVCE.39:
The salvation of the righteous is from the LORD;
he is their refuge in the time of trouble.
NRSVCE.40:
The LORD helps them and rescues them;
he rescues them from the wicked, and saves them,
because they take refuge in him.

NET.1: Do not fret when wicked men seem to succeed! Do not envy evildoers!
NET.2: For they will quickly dry up like grass, and wither away like plants.
NET.3: Trust in the LORD and do what is right! Settle in the land and maintain your integrity!
NET.4: Then you will take delight in the LORD, and he will answer your prayers.
NET.5: Commit your future to the LORD! Trust in him, and he will act on your behalf.
NET.6: He will vindicate you in broad daylight, and publicly defend your just cause.
NET.7: Wait patiently for the LORD! Wait confidently for him! Do not fret over the apparent success of a sinner, a man who carries out wicked schemes!
NET.8: Do not be angry and frustrated! Do not fret! That only leads to trouble!
NET.9: Wicked men will be wiped out, but those who rely on the LORD are the ones who will possess the land.
NET.10: Evil men will soon disappear; you will stare at the spot where they once were, but they will be gone.
NET.11: But the oppressed will possess the land and enjoy great prosperity.
NET.12: Evil men plot against the godly and viciously attack them.
NET.13: The Lord laughs in disgust at them, for he knows that their day is coming.
NET.14: Evil men draw their swords and prepare their bows, to bring down the oppressed and needy, and to slaughter those who are godly.
NET.15: Their swords will pierce their own hearts, and their bows will be broken.
NET.16: The little bit that a godly man owns is better than the wealth of many evil men,
NET.17: for evil men will lose their power, but the LORD sustains the godly.
NET.18: The LORD watches over the innocent day by day and they possess a permanent inheritance.
NET.19: They will not be ashamed when hard times come; when famine comes they will have enough to eat.
NET.20: But evil men will die; the LORD’s enemies will be incinerated — they will go up in smoke.
NET.21: Evil men borrow, but do not repay their debt, but the godly show compassion and are generous.
NET.22: Surely those favored by the LORD will possess the land, but those rejected by him will be wiped out.
NET.23: The LORD grants success to the one whose behavior he finds commendable.
NET.24: Even if he trips, he will not fall headlong, for the LORD holds his hand.
NET.25: I was once young, now I am old. I have never seen a godly man abandoned, or his children forced to search for food.
NET.26: All day long he shows compassion and lends to others, and his children are blessed.
NET.27: Turn away from evil! Do what is right! Then you will enjoy lasting security.
NET.28: For the LORD promotes justice, and never abandons his faithful followers. They are permanently secure, but the children of evil men are wiped out.
NET.29: The godly will possess the land and will dwell in it permanently.
NET.30: The godly speak wise words and promote justice.
NET.31: The law of their God controls their thinking; their feet do not slip.
NET.32: Evil men set an ambush for the godly and try to kill them.
NET.33: But the LORD does not surrender the godly, or allow them to be condemned in a court of law.
NET.34: Rely on the LORD! Obey his commands! Then he will permit you to possess the land; you will see the demise of evil men.
NET.35: I have seen ruthless evil men growing in influence, like a green tree grows in its native soil.
NET.36: But then one passes by, and suddenly they have disappeared! I looked for them, but they could not be found.
NET.37: Take note of the one who has integrity! Observe the godly! For the one who promotes peace has a future.
NET.38: Sinful rebels are totally destroyed; evil men have no future.
NET.39: But the LORD delivers the godly; he protects them in times of trouble.
NET.40: The LORD helps them and rescues them; he rescues them from evil men and delivers them, for they seek his protection.
